attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who attacks from small groups in final 1-6km, particularly effective on hard stages with solo or small group finishes. Shows unexpected sprint capacity when reaching finale with reduced numbers.
team_tactics: Primary role as high-altitude domestique delivering GC leaders deep into final climbs. When given freedom, exploits breakaway positions on hardest stages rather than designated sprint stages.
terrain_preference: Excels on mountainous terrain with altitude finishes and on exceptionally hard one-day parcours. Shows versatility matching pure climbers (Alaphilippe) while retaining sprint capacity in reduced groups.
vulnerability: Tactically naive in sprint execution, repeatedly leading out final 500m and being passed. Inferior pure finishing speed when facing specialist sprinters despite barrier/positioning craft.
key_patterns: Attacks from small groups (4 riders or fewer) in final 1km, documented twice. (2)
Elite domestique work pacing GC leaders deep into altitude climbs, reducing peloton significantly. (2)
Reaches podium positions but tactically gives away wins through premature sprint leadouts. (3)
Targets breakaway success on explicitly hardest stages rather than easier opportunities. (2)
Shows unusual sprint capacity for climbing domestique role, creating dual-threat breakaway profile. (3)
form_trajectory: neutral: Last 2022 observation shows maintained breakaway endurance (solo to 6km) but no recent evidence of previous sprint finishing ability or high-mountain domestique deployment
